body_txt: A character sees a friend or family member or encounters a stranger who is down on his luck, probably homeless and maybe jobless, and he has no place to go. The person may be an orphaned child or a teen thrown out of their home, or someone who's faced a major loss, such as divorce or the death of a spouse. The Good Samaritan takes pity on him/her and invites them to live in his home until he can bounce back and find his own place. In some cases, the person is treated like a member of the family and a close bond develops. Sometimes, though, the guest overstays their welcome, which may be Played for Laughs . This is Truth in Television for a lot of people, at least with helping friends and family. Compare Recruited from the Gutter , in which one character might invite another character to live with them in order to gain access to that character's skills, and Happily Adopted , which involves formal adoption. Might be related to Staying with Friends . Can sometimes lead to the sap becoming The Thing That Would Not Leave . Financial Test of Friendship is a subtrope for when this occurs with a rich person who has lost their wealth. See also Pretty Freeloaders .
